why_this_milestone_exists.purpose: Record the new Kangaroo policy-debug renderer and the confirmed ladder-overlap failure mode in policy_v6.
why_this_milestone_exists.trigger: Video inspection suggested that the policy sends UP when the kangaroo overlaps the ladder by only one pixel, causing repeated jump/climb confusion.

confirmed_failure_mode.summary: The policy's on-ladder predicate is too permissive. It treats a one-pixel horizontal bounding-box overlap as enough to issue UP.

reward_event.interpretation: The monkey punch overrides the climb/path action. The policy aligns near the ladder as a side effect of approaching the monkey, then the weak on-ladder predicate keeps retriggering UP.

high_budget_evaluation.interpretation: Increasing the evaluation horizon did not reveal hidden progress beyond the one-event 200-point plateau.

parameter_only_retune.interpretation: A smaller API-free parameter-only retune did not improve policy_v6 beyond 200.

interpretation: The bottleneck is now more specific than just post-200 route planning: the current perception helper for being on a ladder is wrong.
The policy needs explicit parametric perception functions such as ladder overlap fraction, player-center band, feet-to-ladder-bottom window, and punch distance.
Those perception parameters should be tested and then fixed or narrowed before optimizing higher-level route logic, otherwise CMA-ES has too many coupled parameters to search effectively.
The debug renderer implements the requested right-side action/decision display and exports a JSON trace so individual frames can be inspected without guessing why an action was chosen.

rq_relevance.RQ1: Explains why the current Kangaroo policy remains stuck at 200 despite longer evaluation and parameter retuning.
rq_relevance.RQ2: Strongly relevant: the score comes from brittle local geometry and monkey-punch priority, not reliable route completion.
rq_relevance.RQ4: Parameter-only CMA-ES on policy_v6 did not improve beyond 200 in this smaller retune.

next_steps.step: Build or prompt for a minimal policy_v6-style patch with a parametric _is_on_ladder helper.
next_steps.why: The current helper issues UP with only one pixel of overlap. The fix should require stronger overlap or center alignment before climbing.

next_steps.step: Use the debug renderer after any Kangaroo change before paying for another LLM run.
next_steps.why: It shows action, decision reason, ladder overlap, and priority overrides frame by frame.

next_steps.step: Separate low-level perception parameters from high-level route parameters.
next_steps.why: Once on-ladder and punch-distance predicates are validated, they should be fixed or tightly bounded so CMA-ES can search the remaining policy more effectively.
